title: AP 宏观经济 2023年样题2
description: AP AP 宏观经济 2023年样题2

scoring.note: Scoring unavailable until answer keys are imported.

questions.prompt: Which of the following typically occurs during an expansionary phase of a business cycle?

options.content: Nominal interest rates decrease.
Income taxes decrease.
The price level decreases.
Government transfer payments increase.
Employment increases.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Economic growth refers to an increase in which of the following?

options.content: Government spending
Consumption spending
Nominal gross domestic product
Potential real gross domestic product
Household wealth

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: If both the nominal interest rate and the expected inflation rate increase, what will happen to the real interest rate?

options.content: It will increase because the expected inflation rate has increased.
It will increase because the nominal interest rate has increased.
It will increase if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.
It will decrease because the nominal interest rate has increased.
It will decrease if the expected inflation rate increases by more than the nominal interest rate.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following statements is true about an expansionary fiscal policy?

options.content: It decreases demand for loanable funds.
It decreases the equilibrium price level.
It decreases the equilibrium real interest rate.
It increases aggregate demand.
It increases the money supply.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following will most likely result from deflation?

options.content: Increased nominal interest rates
Increased business profits
Increased real value of fixed incomes
Decreased purchasing power of cash
Decreased real wealth

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a defining characteristic of a fractional reserve banking system?

options.content: The existence of a central bank with a monopoly on money creation
The use of paper money backed by a commodity such as gold or silver
The fact that banks retain an amount of bank reserves that is less than the amount of customer demand deposits
The requirement that banks maintain a certain percentage of their reserves as a deposit in an account at the central bank
The regulations that separate investment banking from commercial banking

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: A negative aggregate supply shock will result in which of the following in the short run?

options.content: An increase in the price level and a decrease in the unemployment rate
A decrease in the price level and an increase in the unemployment rate
A decrease in both the price level and real output
An increase in both the price level and real output
An increase in both the price level and the unemployment rate

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following combinations of fiscal and monetary policies will correct a severe recession?

options.content: Increasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates
Increasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and decreasing administered interest rates
Decreasing income tax rates and increasing the policy rate

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Which of the following would decrease the United States net exports to South Korea?

options.content: South Korean computer companies sell more computers to the United States.
South Korean insurance companies provide fewer services to the United States.
South Koreans spend more vacations in the United States.
United States banks provide more banking services to South Korea.
United States smartphone companies sell more smartphones to South Korea.

questions.prompt: An increase in the purchases of newly constructed houses will result in which of the following?

options.content: Aggregate demand will decrease as a result of a decrease in the price level.
Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in investment spending.
Aggregate demand will increase as a result of an increase in exports.
Aggregate demand will not change, since consumer spending has not changed.
Aggregate demand will not change, since investment spending has not changed.

questions.explanation: Answer key not available yet for this imported exam.

questions.prompt: Suppose that a country's nominal gross domestic product (GDP) was $1,000 in year 1 and $2,000 in year 2. If year 1 is the base year and real GDP in year 2 was $1,000, which of the following is true?

options.content:
A.

Prices fell by 50% between year 1 and year 2.

B.

Prices doubled between year 1 and year 2.

C.

Prices remained the same between year 1 and year 2.

D.

More goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

E.

Fewer goods and services were produced in year 2 than in year 1.

options.content: Sell government bonds
Decrease the discount rate

Increase government spending

questions.prompt: Which of the following types of unemployment is caused by a recession?

options.content: Hidden
Frictional

questions.prompt: If the United States government increases deficit spending, which of the following will occur as a result of the change in the interest rate?

options.content: The United States dollar will appreciate in foreign exchange markets.
Household savings in the United States will decrease.
The United States exports will increase.
The demand for United States dollars will decrease.

options.content: a rightward shift in the aggregate demand curve
a leftward shift in the aggregate demand curve

options.content: British investors will buy more United States securities.
British exports to the United States will increase.
The supply of dollars will decrease.

C.

D.

questions.prompt: Assume that Country X and Country Y are trading partners. If the average income in Country X increases, which of the following will occur in the foreign exchange market?

options.content: The demand for Country X’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will depreciate.
The demand for Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The supply of Country X’s currency will decrease, and Country X’s currency will appreciate.
The supply of Country Y’s currency will increase, and Country Y’s currency will depreciate.

questions.prompt: Assuming a banking system with limited reserves, which of the following is a monetary policy aimed at increasing the equilibrium interest rate in the money market?

options.content: Raising taxes
Lowering the discount rate
Lowering the federal funds rate
Selling bonds on the open market
Lowering the required reserve ratio

questions.prompt: Which of the following monetary and fiscal policy mixes will reduce unemployment?

options.content: Decreasing interest on reserves and increasing taxes

Increasing interest on reserves and decreasing government spending
Increasing interest on reserves and increasing taxes

questions.prompt: Which of the following is correct according to the circular flow model of an economy?

options.content: Taxes received from the public equal government spending.
Imports equal exports.
Total spending equals total income.
Consumption plus saving equals investment.
Saving plus investment equals imports plus exports.

questions.prompt: If the short-run aggregate supply curve is upward sloping, which of the following will cause inflation?

An increase in aggregate demand
A decrease in aggregate demand

questions.prompt: Sam and Bill run a leaf raking and lawn mowing business. In one day, Sam can rake 3 lawns or mow 5 lawns whereas Bill can rake 2 lawns or mow 6 lawns. Which of the following correctly describes Sam’s and Bill’s absolute and comparative advantages?

options.content: Sam has a comparative advantage in raking and an absolute advantage in mowing.
Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in raking.
Sam has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in mowing.
Bill has a comparative advantage in mowing and an absolute advantage in mowing.
Bill has a comparative advantage in raking and an absolute advantage in mowing.
